title: How Carbon Dating Works
script_summary: An intriguing explanation of carbon dating and its limitations.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you a tiny atom can unlock the secrets of the past?
Meet Carbon-14, a radioactive isotope formed in the atmosphere.
It decays at a known rate. This decay acts like a clock for once-living materials.
But it only works for items up to about 50,000 years old. Beyond that? It’s a mystery.
Archaeologists and physicists rely on this method to uncover our history.
Want to explore more science mysteries? Hit that follow button!
